Wood , Stanford University, California Show more authors * You may already have access via personal or institutional login * + Check access Check if you have access via personal or institutional login * Select format Digital US$55.00 * Publisher: Cambridge University Press Publication date: 05 July 2013 13 January 1998 ISBN: 9780511804649 DOI: Dimensions: Weight & Pages: Dimensions: Weight & Pages: Subjects: Eighteenth-Century Philosophy , Philosophy Texts , Philosophy Series: The Cambridge Edition of the Works of Immanuel Kant Collection: Works of Immanuel Kant You may already have access via personal or institutional login * + Check access Check if you have access via personal or institutional login Selected: Digital Add to cart View cart [Buy from Cambridge.org](https://www.cambridge.org/core_title/gb/129673) Subjects: Eighteenth-Century Philosophy , Philosophy Texts , Philosophy Series: The Cambridge Edition of the Works of Immanuel Kant Collection: Works of Immanuel Kant Information Information Contents Metrics Accessibility ### Book description This entirely new translation of Critique of Pure Reason is the most accurate and informative English translation ever produced of this epochal philosophical text. Though its simple and direct style will make it suitable for all new readers of Kant, the translation displays an unprecedented philosophical and textual sophistication that will enlighten Kant scholars as well. This translation recreates as far as possible a text with the same interpretative nuances and richness as the original. The extensive editorial apparatus includes informative annotation, detailed glossaries, an index, and a large-scale general introduction in which two of the world's preeminent Kant scholars provide both a succinct summary of the structure and argument of the Critique and a detailed account of its long and complex genesis. * * + Aa Reduce text + Aa Enlarge text ##### Refine List * Classifications ## Actions for selected content: Select all | Deselect all * View selected items * Save to my bookmarks * Export citations * Download PDF (zip) * Save to Kindle * Save to Dropbox * Save to Google Drive * # Save content to To save content items to your account, please confirm that you agree to abide by our usage policies.
